body:
## Summary
- publish Sepo preview URLs through GitHub Deployment statuses instead of PR comments
- use the resolved Sepo auth token when it can create deployments, with GITHUB_TOKEN fallback
- mark matching preview deployments inactive when the PR closes
- update README preview docs

## Validation
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'\n- git diff --check

comments.body: [vc]: #sKRc/wq2F6wv+Qa1StZzmObZI4sUNuLAjAr1Mml85Z0=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/2UBCpYv5n2Qc23NC1rnZSMxtevTN) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-agent-preview-depl-3420fd-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-agent-preview-depl-3420fd-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 10, 2026 11:44pm |
@sepo-agent /answer should we consider all the implementation in `.agent` with the corresponding cli server?

Yes, but not **all** of it.

I’d move the reusable GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` behind a CLI, because PR #22 currently embeds fairly complex deployment creation, token fallback, and inactive-marking shell directly in the workflow: `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml` lines [64-154](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L64-L154) and [179-213](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L179-L213). That matches the existing `.agent` pattern where deterministic GitHub operations are wrapped in CLI entrypoints, e.g. comments in [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts) and shared `gh` helpers in [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts#L1-L4).

I would **not** move the site build, artifact upload, or preview-server OIDC deploy/teardown call just for purity. Those are workflow/server integration steps, and README already describes them as the workflow asking `preview-api.sepo.sh` to publish the artifact: [README.md#L209](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/README.md#L209).

Suggested plan:

1. Add a `.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts` CLI for creating the deployment and success status.
2. Add a `.agent/src/cli/inactivate-preview-deployments.ts` CLI for finding Sepo preview deployments by PR/SHA and marking them inactive.
3. Put payload construction and `gh api` calls in reusable `.agent/src/github.ts` helpers.
4. Replace the large inline workflow shell blocks with short `node .agent/dist/cli/...` calls.
5. Add focused CLI tests for token fallback, payload shape, matching by payload PR/SHA, and no-match teardown behavior.
6. Keep the preview server `curl` deploy/teardown calls in the workflow unless we decide to make preview-server integration reusable across multiple workflows.

So: yes, we should consider `.agent` CLI coverage for the deterministic GitHub-side implementation, but keep workflow-only and preview-server plumbing outside unless reuse pressure appears.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

@sepo-agent /fix-pr

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/preview-deployment-status-events`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

<!-- sepo-agent-fix-pr-status -->

- Moved preview Deployment/status creation and inactive marking into `.agent` CLIs backed by shared `github.ts` helpers.
- Updated `agent-site-preview.yml` to call the CLIs while keeping site build, artifact upload, and preview-server `curl` calls in the workflow.
- Added focused CLI tests for payload shape, token fallback, payload PR/SHA matching, and no-match teardown behavior.
- Checks passed: `.agent` `npm run build`; `node --test dist/__tests__/preview-deployment-cli.test.js`; workflow YAML parse; `git diff --check`.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Put shared capabilities in runtime | pass | 7/7 | Reusable Deployment/status behavior now lives in `.agent/src/github.ts` helpers and two `.agent` CLIs, while the workflow only calls the CLIs: [github.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/github.ts#L169-L456), [agent-site-preview.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L64-L75). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change extends the existing shared GitHub helper module instead of embedding `gh api` logic in workflow shell, and uses the existing setup-agent-runtime action: [github.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/github.ts#L18-L33), [agent-site-preview.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L25-L27). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The CLIs consume separate validated fields for repo, PR, SHA, URL, head ref, run URL, environment, and tokens rather than overloading a command string: [publish CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts#L12-L57), [inactive CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/cli/inactivate-preview-deployments.ts#L7-L37). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Deployment/status creation errors are surfaced as GitHub Actions warnings and final errors, and no-match teardown state is explicit rather than silently pretending work happened: [publish CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts#L27-L73), [inactive CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/cli/inactivate-preview-deployments.ts#L39-L49). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README preview docs now name `agent-site-preview.yml`, Deployment status events, token fallback, and inactive teardown behavior: [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/README.md#L207-L209). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to the preview deployment runtime helpers, two CLIs, focused tests, the preview workflow wiring, and the relevant README sentence. |

## Notes

- The PR has no parent/tracking issue reference in the body, but the current PR discussion includes a `/answer` recommendation to move deterministic GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` CLIs while keeping preview-server plumbing in the workflow; this diff follows that plan.
- Focused tests cover publish payload shape, Sepo-token success, fallback to `GITHUB_TOKEN`, inactive matching by PR/SHA, and no-match teardown behavior: [preview-deployment-cli.test.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/__tests__/preview-deployment-cli.test.ts#L26-L279).
- Validation from PR head `ab7ae58`: after `npm ci`, `.agent` `npm test` passed 696 tests; workflow YAML parse passed; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #22 replaces generated Sepo preview PR comments with GitHub Deployment/status events. It uses the resolved Sepo auth token when possible, falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN`, marks preview deployments inactive on close, and updates the README. A follow-up `/fix-pr` moved reusable GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` CLIs while leaving preview-server deploy/teardown plumbing in the workflow. The current architecture matches the PR discussion plan, but two close-path correctness issues remain.

## Review
The implementation direction is sound and the rubrics review found no architecture or workflow-policy blockers. The unresolved issues are concrete, branch-fixable warnings around teardown scoping and cleanup reliability.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Teardown matching can inactivate another PR | WARNING | `previewDeploymentMatches` matches by PR number or head SHA, so closing one PR can mark another PR’s deployment inactive if both point at the same commit. |
| GitHub cleanup can be skipped after teardown failure | WARNING | The inactive-status CLI runs after the preview-server teardown step, so a transient teardown error prevents GitHub Deployment cleanup from running. |

## Progress
- The prior `/fix-pr` moved Deployment/status creation and inactive marking into `.agent` CLIs backed by shared helpers.
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific blocking or warning findings.
- Posted inline comments for both unresolved warnings: `.agent/src/github.ts:438` and `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml:110`.
- No existing inline comments or review threads needed replies, minimization, or resolution.
- Info-only notes about setup cost, `auto_inactive`, and minor CLI ergonomics are not required branch changes.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Teardown matching can inactivate another PR</summary>

**Cause:** [`previewDeploymentMatches`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.agent/src/github.ts#L438-L439) returns true when either `pull_request` matches or `head_sha` matches. Two PRs can legitimately share a commit SHA.

**Candidate solutions:** Match close-time cleanup by `payload.pull_request === prNumber`. If SHA fallback is needed for legacy malformed payloads, only use it when `pull_request` is absent rather than conflicting.

**Comments:** Both non-rubric reviewers flagged this; the current test also asserts the risky cross-PR match behavior and should be updated with the predicate.

</details>

<details>
<summary>GitHub cleanup can be skipped after teardown failure</summary>

**Cause:** [`Teardown preview`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/ab7ae58ebb254d24522748575bc1503815d94e23/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L96-L110) exits nonzero before checkout/setup/mark-inactive steps run when the preview server returns a non-200/404 response.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `if: always()` to the checkout, setup, and mark-inactive steps, or run GitHub inactive marking before the preview-server teardown.

**Comments:** One reviewer flagged this and no current review artifact disagrees.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: both unresolved warnings require small, concrete branch changes that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Narrow preview deployment teardown matching so a conflicting `pull_request` cannot be overridden by a matching `head_sha`.
- [ ] Ensure GitHub inactive Deployment status cleanup runs even when preview-server teardown fails.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Put shared capabilities in runtime | pass | 7/7 | GitHub Deployment/status behavior is in shared `.agent/src/github.ts` helpers and CLIs, while the workflow calls `node .agent/dist/cli/...`: [github.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.agent/src/github.ts#L169-L463), [workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L64-L75). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change extends the existing `.agent/src/github.ts` GitHub helper surface and uses the repo’s `setup-agent-runtime` action instead of leaving complex `gh api` shell in the workflow: [workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L25-L27). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The CLIs validate separate env fields for repo, PR, SHA, URL, head ref, run URL, environment, and tokens rather than parsing a compound command string: [publish CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts#L38-L57), [inactive CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.agent/src/cli/inactivate-preview-deployments.ts#L22-L37). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Publish failures emit Actions warnings/errors, no-match teardown is explicit, and GitHub inactive cleanup now runs on failed teardown unless the job was cancelled: [publish CLI](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts#L59-L73), [teardown workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L100-L117). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README preview docs now name `agent-site-preview.yml`, Deployment status events, token fallback, and inactive teardown behavior: [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/README.md#L207-L209). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to preview Deployment helpers/CLIs/tests, the preview workflow wiring, and the relevant README sentence. |

## Notes

- The PR body has no parent/tracking issue reference. The relevant linked PR discussion asked to move deterministic GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` CLIs while leaving preview-server plumbing in the workflow; the current diff follows that plan.
- The earlier review warnings are resolved in current head `8caf421`: teardown matching uses `pull_request` when present with SHA fallback only for legacy payloads, and cleanup steps use `if: !cancelled()` after preview-server teardown.
- Focused tests cover publish payloads, token fallback, PR-vs-SHA matching, legacy SHA fallback, and no-match teardown behavior: [preview-deployment-cli.test.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7/.agent/src/__tests__/preview-deployment-cli.test.ts#L26-L283).
- Validation checked during this review: `git diff --check origin/main...origin/pr/22` passed; workflow YAML parse passed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 8caf421960c9278b53e2adcbd7ff42357d0f53d7 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #22 replaces generated Sepo preview PR comments with GitHub Deployment/status events. It publishes preview readiness with a resolved Sepo auth token when possible, falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN`, marks matching deployments inactive on PR close, and updates the README. A follow-up `/fix-pr` moved reusable Deployment/status logic into `.agent` CLIs while keeping preview-server deploy/teardown plumbing in the workflow. The current review is a follow-up after earlier warning comments, and current head `8caf421` addresses those warnings.

## Review
Claude, Codex, and the rubrics reviewer agree there are no current blocking or warning findings. The remaining notes are informational implementation tradeoffs, not required branch changes.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | Current head resolves the prior teardown-scope and cleanup-order warnings; no reviewer raised a remaining required code change. |

## Progress
- The PR body has no parent/tracking issue reference; the relevant PR discussion asked to move deterministic GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` CLIs, and the current diff follows that plan.
- Prior teardown matching risk is resolved: PR-number-bearing payloads match by PR number, with SHA fallback only for legacy payloads without `pull_request`.
- Prior cleanup-order risk is resolved: teardown cleanup steps now use `if: ${{ !cancelled() }}` so GitHub inactive cleanup still runs after preview-server failure.
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific blocking or warning findings.
- Marked two older same-agent inline review comments as outdated; no new inline comments were posted and no threads were resolved because the fetched threads were not viewer-resolvable.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable branch work remains; remaining notes are informational.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #22.

no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #22 history, review comments, trust signals, diff scope, and the relevant existing rubrics. The durable lessons are already covered by active rubrics like `reuse-existing-code`, `shared-capabilities-in-runtime`, and `surface-stateful-failures`. The detailed feedback was bot-authored and advisory, with no new trusted maintainer preference beyond those existing rubrics.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

title: Use deployment events for Sepo previews
